operating_state: Deere achieved record net sales of $61.3 billion in fiscal 2023, up 16% driven by strong demand across all segments and 12% price realization. Net income attributable to Deere reached $10.2 billion compared to $7.1 billion in the prior year, with operating profit expanding to $13.0 billion from $9.5 billion. Production & precision agriculture led growth while construction & forestry and small agriculture & turf delivered solid increases. Backlog orders decreased across all segments as demand normalized from elevated prior-year levels.
cost_structure: Cost of sales ratio improved 580 basis points to 67.9% as price realization exceeded production cost increases, with material costs rising moderately through Q3 before declining in Q4. Operating expenses increased supporting technology investments and Smart Industrial Operating Model implementation. Research and development expenses rose 14% supporting precision agriculture and autonomous vehicle technologies. Interest expense surged to $2.5 billion from $1.1 billion due to rising rates across the portfolio.
material_events: Deere completed acquisitions totaling $307 million to advance autonomous and precision spraying capabilities. The company executed $7.2 billion in share repurchases compared to $3.6 billion in 2022, with board authorizing $18.0 billion in December 2022. Russian operations were fully divested recognizing an $18 million loss. Brazil tax ruling in Q3 2023 resulted in $243 million reduction in income tax provision.
macro_exposures: Rising Federal Reserve interest rates compressed financial services spreads by $170 million after-tax as borrowing costs rose faster than financing receivable yields. California regulations prohibiting small off-road spark-ignition engines under 25 horsepower take effect in 2024, impacting turf care products. Foreign exchange volatility resulted in $122 million loss in 2023 with Argentine peso exposure limited after hedging.
forward_outlook: Management committed to offering electric options in each turf and compact utility tractor product family by 2026 to comply with emissions regulations. Strategic focus continues on Leap Ambitions framework through 2030, emphasizing precision agriculture adoption and autonomous machine solutions. Capital allocation priorities include technology development investments, dividend sustainability at $5.05 per share, and opportunistic share repurchases with $13.0 billion remaining under authorization.
uncertainty_profile: Collective bargaining agreements covering U.S. employees expire between 2024-2027, with UAW agreement covering 11,500 workers expiring November 2027. Deere faces consolidated multidistrict class action lawsuit alleging repair service monopolization and ongoing FTC investigation into repair practices. Unrecognized tax benefits totaled $907 million with potential tax rate impact, while financing receivables portfolio of $51.2 billion maintains $175 million credit loss allowance.
